summary:
The agent correctly identifies that both darunavir and DN501 act via inhibition of HIV-1 protease, disrupting the maturation of infectious virus particles. Despite differing metabolic effects such as hyperglycemia, this core mechanistic similarity underpins their shared efficacy profile within antiretroviral treatment regimens.  

(Note: Summary does not include speculative details beyond what was explicitly stated.)
